# x402

> HTTP 402 payment server. AI agents pay native USDC on Base via EIP-3009 `transferWithAuthorization` in exchange for a Sentinel decentralized VPN subscription. One HTTP request, one signature, zero prerequisites ‚Äî no custom contract, no `approve()`, no bridge, no KYC.

The agent signs an EIP-3009 message off-chain. The self-hosted facilitator settles USDC on Base (~2s). The server provisions a Sentinel subscription share + a fee grant in one atomic TX. The agent then opens a direct WireGuard/V2Ray tunnel to a node. The operator never sees the tunnel or the traffic.

## Prerequisites

The agent needs exactly one thing before calling x402: an EVM private key with at least $1.00 USDC on Base (chainId 8453). Everything else ‚Äî Sentinel wallet, P2P gas, node selection, tunnel keys ‚Äî is provisioned by x402 or generated locally by `blue-js-sdk/ai-path`.

**Payment is identical on every platform.** What differs is *connecting* the tunnel after the server returns 200. There are two equally-supported connect paths ‚Äî pick by OS:

- **macOS & Linux (easiest):** WireGuard already ships with the OS, so there is nothing to install for the tunnel itself. Use the native `sentinel-dvpncli` (one `go install`), then drive it with three flag-only commands ‚Äî see **"macOS & Linux ‚Äî native CLI connect"** below. No admin/sudo for V2Ray; WireGuard may need sudo to bring an interface up, same as any WireGuard client. **Only exception: Fedora** ‚Äî its SELinux blocks VPN interfaces and cannot be worked around programmatically; macOS, Ubuntu, Debian and other mainstream Linux are fine.
- **Windows (heavier):** run `await setup()` from `blue-js-sdk/ai-path` once on a fresh machine before `connect()`. It auto-downloads V2Ray 5.2.1 to `~/.sentinel-sdk/bin` (no admin) and detects/auto-installs WireGuard (the MSI install needs admin). It returns `{ ready, capabilities, recommended, issues }` ‚Äî if `capabilities` includes `v2ray`, you can connect with zero manual installs. See **"Windows ‚Äî JS-SDK connect"** below.

Both paths consume the exact same 200 response (`subscriptionId`, `planId`, `feeGranter`, `nodeAddress`) and end in the same `MsgStartSession` + direct WireGuard/V2Ray handshake.

How to fund a fresh EVM key with USDC on Base:
- **Coinbase / Coinbase CDP**: withdraw USDC and pick "Base" as the network. ~30 seconds, no bridge fee.
- **Any CEX that supports Base withdrawals** (Binance, Kraken, OKX, Bybit): same flow.
- **Bridge from Ethereum L1**: use `bridge.base.org` (canonical bridge) or any third-party bridge (Across, Stargate). USDC on Base is native after Circle's CCTP ‚Äî no wrapped tokens.
- **DEX swap on Base**: if the wallet already holds ETH on Base, swap to USDC on Uniswap or Aerodrome.

Confirm with: `curl https://api.basescan.org/api?module=account&action=tokenbalance&contractaddress=0x833589fCD6eDb6E08f4c7C32D4f71b54bdA02913&address=<your_addr>`

No ETH is required for the agent's wallet ‚Äî EIP-3009 `transferWithAuthorization` is gasless from the agent's side (the facilitator pays Base gas).

## Live endpoints

Base URL: `https://x402.sentinel.co`

Paid (HTTP 402, body `{ "sentinelAddr": "sent1...", "country": "DE" }` ‚Äî `country` optional):
- POST `/vpn/connect/1day` ‚Äî $0.033 USDC, 1 day
- POST `/vpn/connect/7days` ‚Äî $0.233 USDC, 7 days
- POST `/vpn/connect/30days` ‚Äî $1.00 USDC, 30 days

Free:
- GET `/manifest` ‚Äî full machine-readable protocol spec (use this first, ~300 tokens)
- GET `/pricing` ‚Äî human pricing table
- GET `/nodes` ‚Äî VPN nodes in operator plan with live geo data (country, city, protocol, online) and a `byCountry` summary
- GET `/health` ‚Äî uptime
- GET `/agent/:sentinelAddr` ‚Äî subscription + fee-grant status

## Payment

- Network: Base mainnet (`eip155:8453`, chainId 8453)
- Asset: USDC at `0x833589fCD6eDb6E08f4c7C32D4f71b54bdA02913` (6 decimals)
- Scheme: `exact` / EIP-3009 `transferWithAuthorization`
- Gasless for agent on Base settlement (facilitator pays)
- Gasless for agent on Sentinel session start (operator fee-grants)

## Agent flow

1. POST to a paid endpoint with `{ sentinelAddr }` (add `country` to request a node location ‚Äî validated before payment).
2. Server returns 402 with `PAYMENT-REQUIRED` header (amount, asset, payTo, network).
3. Agent signs EIP-3009 locally (EIP-712).
4. Agent resends with `PAYMENT-SIGNATURE` header ‚Äî `@x402/fetch` does this automatically.
5. Facilitator settles USDC on Base.
6. Server submits atomic `MsgShareSubscription` + `MsgGrantAllowance` on Sentinel.
7. Server returns 200 `{ provisioned, subscriptionId, planId, feeGranter, nodeAddress, nodeCountry, nodes[], sentinelTxHash, expiresAt }`.
8. Agent connects, using the path for its OS (both consume the same 200 fields):
   - **macOS/Linux:** `sentinel-dvpncli tx session-start <nodeAddress> --subscription-id <subscriptionId> --tx.fee-granter-addr <feeGranter> --tx.from-name <key>` ‚Üí note the returned session id ‚Üí `sentinel-dvpncli connect <sessionId>`. (See "macOS & Linux ‚Äî native CLI connect".)
   - **Windows:** `setup()` once on a fresh machine, then `connect({ mnemonic, subscriptionId, feeGranter, nodeAddress })`. (See "Windows ‚Äî JS-SDK connect".)
9. `MsgStartSession` on Sentinel ‚Äî gas paid by `feeGranter` (the fee grant the server created in step 6, so the agent's session start costs zero P2P).
10. Direct WireGuard/V2Ray handshake ‚Äî tunnel up. The operator never sees it.

## Reference code ‚Äî payment (all platforms) + Windows connect

The payment half of this snippet is identical on every OS. The trailing `connect()` is the
Windows path (JS-SDK tunnel). On macOS/Linux, stop after you have `provision` and switch to the
CLI recipe in the next section ‚Äî the request/response is byte-for-byte the same.

```typescript
import { x402Client, wrapFetchWithPayment } from '@x402/fetch';
import { ExactEvmScheme } from '@x402/evm/exact/client';
import {
  setup, createWallet, connect,
  rpcQueryNodesForPlan, createRpcQueryClientWithFallback,
} from 'blue-js-sdk/ai-path';

await setup(); // fresh machine: auto-downloads V2Ray, checks WireGuard
const wallet = await createWallet();
const scheme = new ExactEvmScheme({ address, signTypedData });
const client = new x402Client();
client.register('eip155:8453', scheme);
const paidFetch = wrapFetchWithPayment(fetch, client);

const res = await paidFetch('https://x402.sentinel.co/vpn/connect/30days', {
  method: 'POST',
  headers: { 'Content-Type': 'application/json' },
  body: JSON.stringify({ sentinelAddr: wallet.address, country: 'DE' }), // country optional
});
const provision = await res.json();
const { subscriptionId, feeGranter, planId } = provision;

// If the server didn't pre-select a node, query the plan directly
let nodeAddress = provision.nodeAddress;
if (!nodeAddress) {
  const rpc = await createRpcQueryClientWithFallback();
  const { items } = await rpcQueryNodesForPlan(rpc, planId, { status: 1, limit: 50 });
  nodeAddress = items[0]?.address;
}

const vpn = await connect({
  mnemonic: wallet.mnemonic,
  subscriptionId: String(subscriptionId),
  feeGranter,
  nodeAddress,
});
// vpn.connected === true
```

## macOS & Linux ‚Äî native CLI connect

This is the easiest connect path. After the `paidFetch` call above returns `provision`
(`{ subscriptionId, planId, feeGranter, nodeAddress, ... }`), do NOT call the JS `connect()`.
Instead drive Sentinel's native `sentinel-dvpncli` ‚Äî three fully flag-driven commands, no config
files, no interactive prompts when you pass the flags below. WireGuard already ships with macOS
and mainstream Linux, so there is nothing to install for the tunnel itself.

**Install once (Go 1.24+):**

```bash
go install github.com/sentinel-official/sentinel-dvpncli@latest   # binary lands in $(go env GOPATH)/bin
# or from source: git clone https://github.com/sentinel-official/sentinel-dvpncli && cd sentinel-dvpncli && make install
sentinel-dvpncli version
```

**Connect (drive these with the fields from `provision`):**

```bash
# 1. Import the SAME wallet whose sentinelAddr (sent1...) you sent to x402.
#    The mnemonic is read from stdin; --keyring.backend test makes it non-interactive (no OS keychain prompt).
echo "$AGENT_MNEMONIC" | sentinel-dvpncli keys add agent --keyring.backend test
#    (keyring.backend defaults to "os"; use "test" or "file" for scripted/agent use.)

# 2. Start the session against the subscription x402 shared with you.
#    --tx.fee-granter-addr = provision.feeGranter  => the operator pays the P2P gas, you pay zero.
#    Positional arg          = provision.nodeAddress (sentnode1...).
sentinel-dvpncli tx session-start "$NODE_ADDRESS" \
  --subscription-id "$SUBSCRIPTION_ID" \
  --tx.fee-granter-addr "$FEE_GRANTER" \
  --tx.from-name agent \
  --keyring.backend test \
  --rpc.chain-id sentinelhub-2 \
  --output-format json
#    Capture the session id from the tx result / events (it is the session just created on-chain).

# 3. Bring the tunnel up. Takes the SESSION id (not the subscription id).
#    WireGuard nodes may need sudo to create the interface ‚Äî same as any wg client.
sentinel-dvpncli connect "$SESSION_ID"
```

Defaults baked into the CLI (override only if needed): `--rpc.addrs https://rpc.sentinel.co:443`,
`--rpc.chain-id sentinelhub-2`, `--keyring.name sentinel`. Useful extras: `sentinel-dvpncli keys list`,
`sentinel-dvpncli query ...` (sessions/subscriptions/nodes), `sentinel-dvpncli inspect`,
`sentinel-dvpncli tx session-cancel <id>` to tear down. `connect` traps SIGINT/SIGTERM and runs a
clean down sequence on exit.

> **Fedora caveat:** Fedora's SELinux blocks VPN interfaces and an agent cannot bypass it. macOS,
> Ubuntu, Debian, Arch and other mainstream distros work out of the box. This is the only OS x402
> cannot connect on.

## Packages

- `@x402/fetch` ‚Äî wraps `fetch`, auto-signs 402 responses
- `@x402/evm` ‚Äî `ExactEvmScheme` EIP-3009 signer
- `blue-js-sdk/ai-path` ‚Äî Windows connect path: environment setup (`setup` ‚Äî auto-installs tunnel binaries), Sentinel wallet (`createWallet`, `importWallet`), tunnel (`connect`, `disconnect`), and plan/node discovery (`rpcQueryNodesForPlan`, `createRpcQueryClientWithFallback`) ‚Äî WireGuard/V2Ray
- `sentinel-dvpncli` ‚Äî macOS/Linux connect path: native Go CLI (`go install github.com/sentinel-official/sentinel-dvpncli@latest`). Flag-driven `keys` / `tx session-start` / `connect` ‚Äî drives the tunnel without the JS SDK. WireGuard ships with the OS.

## Request schema

```json
{ "sentinelAddr": "sent1...", "country": "DE" }
```

`sentinelAddr` matches `^sent1[0-9a-z]{38}$` (Sentinel bech32). Generate with `createWallet()` from `blue-js-sdk/ai-path`.

`country` is optional ‚Äî ISO 3166-1 alpha-2 code (`DE`) or name (`Germany`). If no online plan node matches, the server returns `COUNTRY_UNAVAILABLE` with `availableCountries` **before** payment. Check `GET /nodes` for the live `byCountry` summary.

## Response schema (200)

```json
{
  "provisioned": true,
  "subscriptionId": 1192288,
  "planId": 42,
  "feeGranter": "sent1...",
  "nodeAddress": "sentnode1...",
  "nodeCountry": "Germany",
  "nodes": ["sentnode1a...", "sentnode1b..."],
  "sentinelTxHash": "...",
  "expiresAt": "2026-06-17T00:00:00Z"
}
```

## Errors

All errors are JSON `{ code, message, nextAction, docs }`. Branch on `code` ‚Äî it is the stable contract. The codes marked pre-payment are returned **before** payment is taken. Beyond that, USDC settlement only happens after a successful 2xx response ‚Äî error responses (including `PROVISIONING_FAILED`) never charge the agent.

| `code` | Status | Pre-payment | Meaning |
|---|---|---|---|
| `MISSING_SENTINEL_ADDR` | 400 | yes | Body did not include `sentinelAddr`. |
| `INVALID_SENTINEL_ADDR` | 400 | yes | `sentinelAddr` does not match `^sent1[0-9a-z]{38}$`. Use `createWallet()` from `blue-js-sdk/ai-path`. |
| `UNKNOWN_TIER` | 404 | yes | Path tier is not `1day` \| `7days` \| `30days`. |
| `COUNTRY_UNAVAILABLE` | 400 | yes | Requested `country` has no online plan node. Response includes `availableCountries` ‚Äî resend with one of those or omit `country`. |
| `CAPACITY_EXHAUSTED` | 503 | yes | Operator cannot provision right now (subscription pool full, operator balance below new-subscription cost). Retry later ‚Äî re-checked every 60s. |
| `PAYMENT_REQUIRED` | 402 | ‚Äî | Sign EIP-3009 `transferWithAuthorization` and resend with the `PAYMENT-SIGNATURE` header. `@x402/fetch` handles this automatically. |
| `PROVISIONING_FAILED` | 500 | ‚Äî | Sentinel TX failed after payment verification. USDC was **not** charged (settlement only happens on 2xx). Safe to retry. |
| `NOT_FOUND` | 404 | ‚Äî | No route matches. See `/manifest`. |
| `INTERNAL_ERROR` | 500 | ‚Äî | Unexpected server error. |

## Security properties

- Agent's Sentinel private key never leaves the agent. Handshake is agent ‚Üî node.
- Agent's EVM key never leaves the agent. Only the EIP-3009 signature is transmitted.
- Self-hosted facilitator option ‚Äî no third party in the payment path.
- Every settlement is a verifiable public Base transaction.
- Operator stores `sentinelAddr` only. No IPs, traffic, or logs.
- Tunnel is end-to-end encrypted (WireGuard or V2Ray).

## Sentinel chain endpoints

`blue-js-sdk` ships verified RPC and LCD fallbacks and rotates through them automatically (`createRpcQueryClientWithFallback()`); add more at runtime with `addRpcEndpoint(url)`.

- RPC: `rpc.sentinel.co:443`, `sentinel-rpc.polkachu.com`, `rpc.mathnodes.com`, `sentinel-rpc.publicnode.com`, `rpc.sentinel.quokkastake.io`, `rpc.sentinel.suchnode.net` (additional public endpoint, suchnode.net)
- LCD (fallback only): `lcd.sentinel.co`, `sentinel-api.polkachu.com`, `api.sentinel.quokkastake.io`, `sentinel-rest.publicnode.com`, `api.sentinel.suchnode.net`

## Quick discovery for agents

Hit `GET /manifest` first. It returns the entire protocol ‚Äî endpoints, pricing in atomic units, request/response schemas, the state machine, and a runnable code example ‚Äî as JSON in ~300 tokens.

## Source

- README: https://github.com/Sentinel-Bluebuilder/x402/blob/master/README.md
- Manifesto: https://github.com/Sentinel-Bluebuilder/x402/blob/master/MANIFESTO.md
- x402 spec: https://github.com/coinbase/x402
- Sentinel: https://sentinel.co
